etymology.description: phonetic 尹 (yǐn, 'to govern') + semantic 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') -> ruler
etymology.interpretation: The character 君(jūn, 'sovereign') combines 尹(yǐn, 'to govern'), suggesting oversight or rule, with 口(kǒu, 'mouth'), which may imply issuing commands, reinforcing the concept of a ruler who governs by decree.

examples.chinese: 作为一国之君(作為一國之君)，他需要兼顾国家大事(他需要兼顧國家大事)。
examples.pinyin: Zuòwéi yī guó zhī jūn, tā xūyào jiāngù guójiā dàshì.
examples.english: As the sovereign of a nation, he needs to manage the affairs of state.

examples.chinese: 君主制在历史上曾经非常普遍(君主制在歷史上曾經非常普遍)。
examples.pinyin: Jūnzhǔzhì zài lìshǐ shàng céngjīng fēicháng pǔbiàn.
examples.english: Monarchies were once very common throughout history.

examples.chinese: 他受封为君，承担起领导的责任(他受封為君，承擔起領導的責任)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shòu fēng wéi jūn, chéngdān qǐ lǐngdǎo de zérèn.
examples.english: He was appointed as a sovereign, taking on the responsibility of leadership.
